blocks.text: The G sensor is built into the VSA modulator control unit. The G sensor detects the road gradient when the vehicle is stopped and outputs it to the PGM-FI ECU. The PGM-FI ECU performs creep torque switching and idling stop control based on G sensor output (A/D) values sent using F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L. If the change in the output voltage of the G sensor is less than the specified value during driving in which front/rear Gs of the vehicle body occur, the PGM-FI ECU judges that a failure has occurred and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning lamp: off, D indicator lamp: off
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed

paddle shift switch car without equipment

blocks.text: When the G sensor output voltage fluctuation range is less than 0.03V after implementing the following driving modes 1 through 4 in order.

items.text: Acceleration: Accelerator pedal opening 1/16 or more, vehicle speed [vehicle speed] exceeding 5 km/h for 1 second or more
Attained vehicle speed: Vehicle speed [vehicle speed] exceeds 20 km/h
Deceleration: With the accelerator pedal fully closed, vehicle speed [vehicle speed] exceeds 5 km/h for at least 1 second.
Depress the brake pedal and turn the select lever to the D position.
Drive until the vehicle speed [vehicle speed] reaches 21 km/h or more. (Acceleration is continued for at least 1 second)
Deceleration/Stop with accelerator pedal fully closed. (Deceleration continues for at least 1 second at a vehicle speed [vehicle speed] in excess of 5 km/h)
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the DTC and freeze data are stored without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating and without the D indicator light flashing.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
